name: sidecar
description: Claude Code plugin pack with strict concept separation. Top-level dirs: hooks/ (PreToolUse·SessionStart auto-behavior), commands/ (/slash-command invoked), skills/ (Skill tool invocable). 1 plugin = 1 concept; no mixing.

plugins.name: commons
plugins.description: UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — injects a cross-project do/dont layer from commons.tape (granular `@D g0..g60` form, no tool/usage) + a live `pool` roster snapshot (~/.pool/pool.json) appended per injection so the host lineup is visible without running `pool list`; UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the layer never fades; SessionStart additionally appends COMMANDS.md (the cross-project slash-command catalog) once per session. Carries LATTICE_POLICY.md (real-limits-first SSOT).

plugins.name: prefs
plugins.description: User language preferences — 3 axes (code authoring · doc authoring · response language to user). UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook auto-injects current values; UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the preference never fades. /prefs show · /prefs code <lang> · /prefs docs <lang> · /prefs response <lang>. Storage at $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/prefs.json. Defaults: code=english, docs=english, response=korean. SSOT for language preferences.

plugins.name: hexa-lsp
plugins.description: Wire the hexa-lang LSP server (`hexa lsp`) for `.hexa` files. LSP config in plugin-root `.lsp.json` (canonical Claude Code filename, dot-prefixed). Minimal: only `.hexa` claimed.

plugins.name: tape-lsp
plugins.description: Wire `tape-lsp` (canonical .tape v1.2 LSP — diagnostics + hover) for `.tape` files. LSP config in plugin-root `.lsp.json` (canonical Claude Code filename, dot-prefixed). Requires `tape-lsp` on PATH (install via `hx install dancinlab/tape`).

plugins.name: project-tape
plugins.description: PreCompact + PostCompact hook — re-injects <project-root>/project.tape as additionalContext so the project's identity + governance survive auto-compaction. No-op when project.tape is absent.

plugins.name: git-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) deny for force-type git push operations only, implemented in hexa-lang (`_git_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`) — `git push --force` / `-f` · `git push --force-with-lease` · `git push <remote> +<refspec>` (refspec-level force). Hook-bypass (--no-verify) NOT blocked (left to user discipline). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: memory-lint
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory for the auto-memory index file (`memory/MEMORY.md`), implemented in hexa-lang (`_memory_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Reads the post-write file when tool_input.file_path ends in `memory/MEMORY.md` and emits non-blocking additionalContext for: entry count > 30 (hot-tier soft cap → migrate older items to sibling MEMORY.archive.md) · any non-comment line > 150 chars (TLDR refactor budget — verb + nouns, push trailing clauses into the sibling `*.md` file). Keeps the always-loaded MEMORY.md dense + greppable as memories accumulate. Advisory-only by design (per guards-narrow-scope: non-blocking additionalContext > deny for hygiene).

plugins.name: inbox-log-lint
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory for the INBOX domain log (`INBOX.log.md`). Fires when tool_input.file_path ends in `INBOX.log.md` and reads the post-write file to emit non-blocking additionalContext when the cross-project handoff log piles up: entry count (`## ` headers) > 20 (soft cap) · fully-resolved entries (>=1 `- [x]`, zero `- [ ]`) > 12 · non-blank lines > 250 — each suggests moving closed handoffs to a sibling `INBOX.archive.log.md` so the active log stays open + recent. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_inbox_log_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Advisory-only by design (per the guards-narrow-scope policy: non-blocking additionalContext > deny for hygiene). Sister of memory-lint (same pattern for memory/MEMORY.md).

plugins.name: sidecar-lint
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) auto-lint that fires on `git commit` in any Claude Code marketplace plugin pack (any repo with .claude-plugin/marketplace.json), implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Non-blocking findings as additionalContext: stale-history patterns in staged diff (commons @D g15) · hardcoded /Users//home/ absolute paths in staged diff (commons @D g13 · sidecar @D s3) · marketplace.json plugin entry ↔ each plugin.json version drift (commons @D g22) · hook .sh missing user-exec bit · missing CHANGELOG.md at repo root (commons @D g29). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: hexa-native
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard block for `.py` / `.sh` writes inside any project rooted at a directory containing a `project.tape` marker (sidecar's canonical project identity file), implemented in hexa-lang (`_hexa_native.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Covers BOTH write channels: structured file tools (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit) and the Bash redirect channel — `> X.py`, `>> X.sh`, heredoc-to-redirect (`cat > X.py << EOF`), and `tee [-a] X.py`, relative targets resolved against the payload `cwd`. Quote-aware; fd-dup (`2>&1`) yields no target; non-write Bash (`python X.py`, `cat X.py`, `chmod X.sh`) passes through. Walks up parent dirs of the target; if a `project.tape` is found, the write is denied with a message stating the same logic IS writable here as `.hexa` (since `.py` / `.sh` are already supported as ai-native English elsewhere). NO opt-out: no env var, no config file, no exception list, no self-exclusion — hard-coded by design. Targets only `.py` / `.sh`; other extensions pass through. Projects without `project.tape` are unaffected.

plugins.name: plist-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit) hard block for `.plist` writes, implemented in hexa-lang (`_plist_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Any target file ending in `.plist` (LaunchAgents · LaunchDaemons · Info.plist · UserDefaults) is denied with a redirecting message — the agent cannot distinguish user-explicit from model-autonomous intent, so all .plist writes are refused globally; if the user truly wants one, they run the command themselves outside the agent path. LaunchAgents / LaunchDaemons are persistence surfaces (auto-launch on login / boot), so silent writes risk installing daemons the user did not ask for. Enforces commons @D g37 (plist user-request only). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: cloud-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw rented-GPU pod dispatch (commons @D g8). Four orthogonal detectors run on every Bash command: (a) pod-CLI + remote-verb pair scan anywhere in the command — runpodctl {exec|ssh|send|receive|send-file|receive-file|send-file-to-pod|receive-file-from-pod|port} · vast(ai) {exec|ssh|scp|copy|attach-ssh|execute}; (b) ssh/scp/rsync/sftp against `*.runpod.io` / `proxy.runpod.net` / `*.vast.ai` hosts; (c) curl/wget/http(ie)/xh against `api.runpod.io` / `api.runpod.ai` / `rest.runpod.io` / `runpod.io/graphql` / `console.vast.ai` / `vast.ai/api` URLs; (d) dispatcher-script content scan — `bash/sh/zsh/dash <path>` reads the script and re-runs detectors (a)/(b)/(c) on its body, closing the workaround of pre-writing a dispatch.sh with raw `runpodctl ssh` and invoking it. Shell separators normalized to whitespace before tokenizing — `cd /x && runpodctl ssh …`, `bash -c '… vast exec …'`, env-prefixes all caught. Pod-lifecycle verbs (create / get / start / stop / remove / show / search / launch / destroy / config / project / version) pass through. Redirects to `hexa cloud {run|nohup|poll|copy-to|copy-from|copy-dir-*|preflight}`. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_cloud_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pod-monitor
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for GPU pod fires (`hexa cloud nohup` / `hexa cloud run`). Emits non-blocking additionalContext reminding the agent of commons @D g57 — three invariants: set `SAVE_POD=1` envar (pod retain when SSH drops), attach the 🛰️ Monitor tool to the streaming log (no `tail -f` / sleep-poll), AND ⏱ estimate walltime up front with pod-hours headroom (undersize → pod auto-killed mid-run → result LOST). Closes the R8a-LOST class. Sister of cloud-guard (which blocks raw runpodctl/vastai); pod-monitor advises on the wrapped `hexa cloud` path. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_pod_monitor.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: ai-api-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw AI-API calls when a sidecar CLI wraps the same operation, implemented in hexa-lang (`_ai_api_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Two deny patterns: (1) `curl`/`wget`/`http`/`httpie` to an AI hostname (`api.openai.com` · `api.anthropic.com` · `fal.run` · `api.replicate.com` · `generativelanguage.googleapis.com`); (2) inline `python(3)?` / `node` / `deno` / `bun` with `-c`/`-e` AND `import` of `openai` / `anthropic` / `fal_client` / `replicate` / `google.generativeai`. Redirects to `/imagine` (image) / `/research` (text) / etc. — commons @D g50 enforcement. General SDK usage in committed code files (no inline `-c`/`-e`) passes through. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: verify-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw verification-tool usage cited as primary evidence, implemented in hexa-lang (`_verify_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Denies first-token ∈ {wolframscript, mathematica, WolframKernel}, and inline-Python verification via `python(3)? -c '...'` containing `import sympy` / `from sympy` / `import pyphi` / `import wolframclient` with a redirecting message pointing to `hexa verify` (commons @D g5). General sympy / wolframclient usage in committed code files passes through — only inline `-c` verification probes (and the standalone Wolfram CLIs) are refused. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pr-cycle
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) PR full-cycle router for `gh pr create`, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pr_cycle.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Quote-aware tokenizer strips single- and double-quoted regions before scanning, then looks for the `gh pr create` 3-gram anywhere — catches bare `gh pr create …` and chained forms like `git push && gh pr create …`, while test payloads with the trigger inside quotes do NOT fire. When matched (and not draft / not already merging), rewrites via the hook `updatedInput` field to append ` && gh pr merge --squash --admin --delete-branch` and, IF cwd is a linked git worktree (not the main one), additionally appends ` && (cd <main-wt> && git worktree remove <this-wt> --force 2>/dev/null && git branch -D <branch> 2>/dev/null || true)` — transparent atomic create → merge → worktree+branch cleanup. The worktree+branch are detected at hook-time (`git rev-parse --show-toplevel` · `git worktree list --porcelain` first entry · `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`), so cleanup targets the exact context the PR was issued from. Enforces commons @D g47 (PR ship discipline — no unmerged stacks, no dead worktrees). Skip conditions: `--draft` flag present · command already contains `gh pr merge`. NO opt-out by design. Also ships a `/pr-cycle` command (manual one-shot trigger): pushes the current branch then `gh pr create --fill` — the hook auto-appends merge + cleanup, so one command does create → merge → clean. Refuses on main/master.

plugins.name: tape-lint
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Edit|Write) deny for `.tape` edits, implemented in hexa-lang (`_tape_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run` — no Python, no shell shim). Four checks, all diff-aware (pre-existing violations grandfathered, only newly-introduced or worsened items block). (1) fields rule — `@D` blocks accept only `do` / `dont`; new `why` · `tool` · `note` · `ref` · `ex` · ... refused. Fires on any `*.tape` path. (2) length cap — `do` / `dont` value > 100 chars refused (split overflow). commons.tape + project.tape only. (3) authoring-language — when `sidecar prefs` `code` axis is `english`, lines newly introducing non-Latin scripts (Hangul · CJK · Hiragana · Katakana) are refused. commons.tape + project.tape only. (4) @I siblings — project.tape's @I block must carry a `siblings` field; dropping it or adding a fresh @I without it is refused (cross-repo network SSOT). project.tape only. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pool-mcp
plugins.description: stdio MCP server exposing pool hosts as Claude Code MCP tools. Two tools: `mcp__pool__on` (run `pool on <host> <command>` and return {stdout, stderr, exit_code, non_claude_load}) and `mcp__pool__list` (read ~/.pool/pool.json and return the roster, pi5-akida excluded by default per sidecar @D s8 unless `include_anima=true`). JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pool_mcp.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Server registered via `mcpServers` field in plugin.json. POC scaffold — single-shot stdin drain (reads to EOF then processes all queued requests); streaming line-reader / per-tool timeouts / fan-out deferred to 0.2.0.

plugins.name: pool-route
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) pool auto-router + SessionStart routing-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pool_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has ≥1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the heavy classifier (make · cargo · go build · swift build · docker build · pytest · nvidia-smi · train · hexa kick/drill/loop/cc · `find $HOME/core/anima` big-tree scan · …) or a root-needing command, it is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field to run on a pool host over ssh — transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. Routing is EXPLICIT-classifier only (0.6.0): the 0.5.7 load-escalation gate (promote any non-trivial cmd to heavy when summed non-claude CPU > 150%) was REMOVED — on a multi-core Mac that threshold trips constantly and shipped local-bound git/hexa-run/smoke commands to Linux hosts where their paths don't exist. Local-bound guard (0.6.0): `git`/`gh` commands and any `/Users/`·`/home/` absolute-path literal always run local. Heavy-classified commands MUST use pool resources: the hook iterates every eligible host through a preflight (workdir present + `hexa <subverb> --help` dispatchable for kick/drill/loop/cc), routes to the first that passes, and if ALL fail emits a `permissionDecision: deny` with a per-host failure breakdown — never silently falls back to local. `hexa kick`/`drill`/`loop`/`cc` get `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/core/hexa-lang`; other hexa commands get `HEXA_LANG=$PWD`. PATH + `HEXA_MODULE_LOADER` env passthrough for bare ssh shells. Each routing decision appends one JSON line to `~/.pool/route-log.jsonl` (capped at 100); SessionStart reads the last 5 back as additionalContext. OS-capability filter + round-robin load balance. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: limit-guard
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Task) hook, implemented in hexa-lang (`_limit_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`) — detects a session/usage-limit signal in a subagent result and injects a non-blocking checkpoint directive: report progress (committed SHAs vs uncommitted), commit + push uncommitted work, write a `.claude/RESUME.md` resume manifest, stop parallel fan-out and switch to sequential commit-per-unit work. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: question
plugins.description: Quick side-question alias for Claude Code's built-in `/btw`. Two trigger commands: `/question <text>` (primary) and `/q <text>` (2-character alias). Same semantics as /btw — answer briefly (1-3 sentences), then resume the main work. Plugin command (markdown only, no script). Triggers: /question · /q · quick question · 잠깐 · 사이드 질문.

plugins.name: check
plugins.description: Task dashboard skill — /check prints current-state status across surfaces in one shot: `<UPPERCASE>.log.md` checkbox tasks (open vs done count + first 10 open items per log), `gh pr list` open PRs in this repo, `git status -sb` (uncommitted / ahead / behind), `git log --oneline -5` (recent merges). Read-only; no side effects. Triggers: /check · 상태 · 체크 · 현황 · status check · dashboard.

plugins.name: end
plugins.description: Session closure safety check — /end prints a single-shot dashboard of dangling residue in the current repo (uncommitted · unpushed · stash · open PRs by `@me` · marketplace.json ↔ plugin.json version drift · merged-but-undeleted local branches · linked git worktrees) with per-item ✓/⚠/○ marks + recommended actions, and a closing ✅ `100% closure — safe to end` verdict only when every line is green. Read-only — no commit / push / branch delete / worktree remove. Pairs with /ship (the actor) — /end is the verifier. Triggers: /end · 끝 · 마무리 · 종료 · 클로저 · close out · wrap up · 100% closure.

plugins.name: all-bg-go
plugins.description: Parallel fan-out trigger — 'all bg go' → plan table + one background Agent per branch in the same message. REACTIVE single fan-out of what the PRIOR turn offered. For a self-generating repeatable loop, use /cycle instead. Skill (natural language) + /all-bg-go command.

plugins.name: cycle
plugins.description: Autonomous work-loop driver. Four commands: /cycle — next-list (enumerate active-domain open milestones; if empty, auto-seed ≤3 from recent-context signals like prior-turn /gap shortlist · user hint · log-tail open thread via `/domain milestone <text>` per item, then re-enumerate; no-signal case stops with steer-options) → dup-race precheck (3-signal grep: INBOX.log.md entry status + merged PR + git log) → parallel-plan table → fan-out (one background Agent per item, same message) → loop. /cycle-full — depletion brainstorm (rounds until no novel ideas, cap 8) on $ARGUMENTS, then the same precheck → plan-table → fan-out → loop tail (phase 0 is one-time per goal; subsequent rounds use plain /cycle). /cycle-loop — thin wrapper that hands /cycle off to the `loop` skill (dynamic ScheduleWakeup pacing) so rounds fire automatically until ideas deplete or user halts. /cycle-full-loop — /cycle-full once (one-time brainstorm + first fan-out) then hands off to /loop /cycle for subsequent rounds. Distinct from all-bg-go (reactive fan-out of PRIOR-turn branches; /cycle self-generates the next batch each round). NL triggers: /cycle · /cycle-full · /cycle-loop · /cycle-full-loop · 사이클 · 계속 진행 · 다음 라운드 · keep cycling · march on.

plugins.name: pool
plugins.description: /pool <args> — wraps the `pool` CLI (host roster + remote exec — minimal, zero deps). Verbs: list · add <host> · on <host> <cmd> · status · install tailscale · rm <host>. NL trigger via skill. Pairs with commons g9 + sidecar's pool-route hook.

plugins.name: cloud
plugins.description: /cloud <args> — wraps `hexa cloud` (runpod / vast.ai dispatch · canonical subcommand form, structured argv — never raw runpodctl/vastai/ssh/scp/curl per commons g8, hard-enforced by the cloud-guard hook). Subverbs: preflight · run · nohup · poll · copy-to · copy-from. preflight = closed-form GPU mem-budget check (refuses out-of-budget specs before pod spinup · no LLM · $0). NL trigger via skill. Pairs with commons g8 + g12.

plugins.name: hexa-help
plugins.description: /hexa-help [verb] — wraps `hexa --help` (no arg, top-level catalog) or `hexa <verb> --help` (verb-specific). Per commons g7. NL trigger via skill.

plugins.name: secret
plugins.description: /secret <args> — wraps the `secret` CLI (macOS Keychain-backed credentials, dancinlab/secret 0.4.0, dual-channel sync). Verbs: get · set · rotate · check · delete · list · service · init [icloud|github <url>] · backup [enable <url>|disable|status] · sync · migrate. Two independent sync channels (iCloud Drive primary + optional private GitHub mirror) push the same encrypted blob — master password is the only decryption secret. Auto-push ON by default once `backup enable` runs (opt out via SECRET_BACKUP_AUTO=0). High-value protection on set (BIP39 wordlist-validated · xprv/WIF/64-hex auto-detect → refuse without --allow-mnemonic + stdin/tty). rotate emits sentinel only.

plugins.name: quota
plugins.description: /quota:quota <args> — Claude account 5h/7d usage limits + multi-account registry + live credential swap + per-account nicknames. Single-dispatch picker entry; verb routed via $ARGUMENTS. Verbs: list (= status = bare · UNIFIED all-accounts table in chat, 6-col English header: Nickname · Email · Session Used · Session Reset · Weekly Used · Weekly Reset, ▓░ 5-slot bar + humanized time-to-reset; the active account is ★ + fetched LIVE (45s cache), every other account shows its last per-account cache, `—` = never fetched) · all (same table, every account fetched LIVE in one shot — slower, rate-bound; the easy toggle to 'refresh everything') · add [<nick>] (snapshot account + capture credentials into backup store · optional nickname like claude1) · nick <ref> [<nick>] (set or clear a nickname for an existing account; bare nick arg = clear) · switch <ref> (live cred swap, verified + rollback on failure) · remove <ref> (rm · delete · del — drop registry row + backup creds, leaves live ~/.claude.json untouched) · refresh [<ref>] (re-fetch usage into per-account cache; renews expired tokens via refreshToken) · autoadd (silent SessionStart-hook path — registers the active account iff not yet in the registry; driven by the quota-autoadd hook plugin so a fresh `claude /login` self-registers next session) · help. The view also auto-registers the live account if untracked (autoadd parity). <ref> = nickname (e.g. claude1) · email · numeric index — lookup order index→nick→email. Live fetch via OAuth usage endpoint (api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage) + auto refresh-token grant (platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token); 45s per-account cache. Per-account OAuth blob backup in macOS keychain svc 'sidecar-quota' / linux ~/.sidecar/quota:quota/creds/<email>.json (0600 + atomic write). own-accounts-serial only — never a multi-tenant router (ToS red-line). Agent SDK credit (dual-bucket since 2026-06-15) is documented as future direction; current release tracks subscription 5h/7d only. ⚠ uses non-public Anthropic OAuth endpoints — may break if rotated.

plugins.name: quota-autoadd
plugins.description: SessionStart hook — silently auto-registers the active Claude account into the quota registry when a fresh `claude /login` introduces an account not yet tracked. Invokes the quota skill's `_quota.hexa autoadd` (idempotent · always exit 0 · prints one line only when a NEW account lands; already-registered sessions are fully silent). The add path also captures the OAuth blob into the backup store so `/quota:quota switch` works for the new account immediately. Pairs with the `quota` skill — quota-autoadd is the default-on registration layer (manual `/quota:quota add` remains). Resolves the quota binary via $HOME/.claude/plugins/cache/sidecar/quota:quota/<version>/bin/_quota.hexa (portable; $HOME-based; picks the latest cached version by semver `sort -V`, not mtime). NO opt-out by design (commons g11).

plugins.name: kick
plugins.description: /kick <natural-language seed> — runs `hexa kick --seed "<seed>"` (hexa-lang gap-breakthrough / discovery engine, aliased to `hexa drill`). All args join into the seed. NL trigger via skill (`kick this`, `돌파해줘`, `discover for`). Pairs with commons g6.

plugins.name: verify
plugins.description: /verify <args> — runs `hexa verify "$@"` (cross-project tier rubric, TECS-L-aligned). Forms: <id> (atlas atom), --expr <fn> <n> <v> (numerical), --fence "<claim>" (honest ⚪), rubric / list. Returns 🔵🟢🟡🟠🔴⚪ tier badge VERBATIM. NL trigger via skill (`verify this`, `확인해`, `검증해`, `맞아?`). Pairs with commons g5.

plugins.name: atlas
plugins.description: /atlas <args> — wraps `hexa atlas` (atlas SSOT surface, PR-only landing per @D g_atlas_binary_builtin). Read verbs: hash · stats [--audit] · lookup [K] <id> · dump [K] [--json] — all resolve against compile-time embedded static_atlas() (zero fs read, ~6594 P/C/L nodes). Write verbs: append-witness --kind <P|C|L|E> --id <id> {--raw <body> | --stdin} (staging shard for merger.hexa::_discover) · register --from-{verify,drill,check} [--auto-pr] (multi-verb DIRECT in-memory node-gen — builds AtlasNode struct-literal IN MEMORY, folds into compiler/atlas/embedded.gen.hexa via DiscoveryEvent → register_from_event sink; no .n6, no markdown intermediate; honest degrade) · pr --staging <file.n6> (the sole landing verb under PR-only policy). NL trigger via skill (`atlas lookup`, `atlas stats`, `아틀라스 조회`, `아틀라스 등록`, `register from drill`).

plugins.name: domain
plugins.description: Maintain UPPERCASE <NAME>.md (snapshot = final-goal milestone checkboxes) + sister <NAME>.log.md (append-only step log) at project root — auto-scaffolds both, defaults NAME to uppercase basename of git root. Verbs: /domain <NAME> (SELECT the session's active domain → later verbs default to it + show a final-goal progress bar `▓▓▓░░ NN% · done/total` from the snapshot's checkboxes) · /domain (show active) · /domain <task> (append `- [x]` to log) · /domain todo <task> (`- [ ]`) · /domain done <match> (flip [ ]→[x]) · /domain new <header>. Progress is final-goal-based (snapshot completion), not log-based — it doesn't drift as the log grows. Path resolution: a domain pair lives EITHER at the repo root (`<NAME>.md`) OR a self-contained folder (`<NAME>/<NAME>.md`) — every verb resolves root → folder-nested → root-default-scaffold (log follows the snapshot's dir), so folder-nested meta-domains no longer regenerate an empty root scaffold each call. The folder-nested fallback only matches when the nested file's first line is the domain header `# <NAME>` (guards against false-matching an unrelated same-named file like a digest on case-insensitive filesystems).

plugins.name: gap
plugins.description: /gap — multi-axis gap exploration. 42 breakthrough-strategy lenses · 8 families (F4 + F6 each have 6 lenses; `occams-razor` lives in both — hypothesis side and design side). Bare /gap = inline-triage + deep-dive only hot families. /gap full = exhaustive 8-subagent fan-out. /gap <scope> targets the sweep. /gap list prints the catalogue. Surfaces + prioritises gaps; never fixes.

plugins.name: inject
plugins.description: Immediately inject the latest sidecar commons.tape + project.tape into the CURRENT session and sync the local install for the next session. /inject (and `/ij` short alias) runs `sidecar sync` (marketplace pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch) AND prints the current commons.tape + (cwd's) project.tape so the model picks them up this turn.

plugins.name: ship
plugins.description: Atomic ship tail for sidecar plugin changes — /ship -m "<msg>" <path>... stages explicit paths (never -A/-u), credential-scans the staged diff (rpa_ · sk- · hf_ · AKIA), commits, pushes origin/<branch>, then runs `sidecar sync` (marketplace pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch). Mechanical tail of @D ship / commons g27; the agent owns version bump + surface lockstep + message FIRST. NL trigger via skill (ship · 배포 · 출시).

plugins.name: bypass
plugins.description: Anti-punt — DEFAULT BEHAVIOR (auto-fires, not opt-in). Universal self-check before any move that hands control back to the user (interactive input · unauthorized destructive · external visible · explicit user-review request); if all NO, just execute. Extensible catalog of patterns: 'next user action:' blocks · 'Should I proceed?' confirmations · 'Want me to check?' deferrals · option-trees with obvious default · over-clarification · defer-by-waiting · excessive recap. Cross-project always-on enforcement in commons.tape ≥0.7.3.

plugins.name: brainstorm
plugins.description: Iterative brainstorming — given a seed, generates ideas in rounds and keeps going until depletion (no genuinely new ideas vs prior rounds; hard cap 8 rounds). For breadth over selection. Skill (natural language) + /brainstorm command.

plugins.name: easy
plugins.description: Easy (friendly) response style — 7-element pattern (icon · name · alias · plain-line · analogy · ASCII · compare). Skill (natural-language trigger) + /easy command. 5 language samples: en · ko · ja · zh · ru.

plugins.name: easy-auto
plugins.description: SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — auto-injects the `easy` (friendly) 7-element response style as additionalContext so the pattern is active from turn 0 without the user calling /easy. Reads ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/styles/easy.<lang>.md (env `EASY_LANG` override, default `ko`); falls back to easy.md (English base) when the lang variant is missing. UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the style never fades; PreCompact + PostCompact survive auto-compaction. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_easy_auto.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Pairs with the `easy` skill (manual /easy trigger remains — easy-auto is the default-on layer). Emergency escape: `EASY_LANG=off`. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: research
plugins.description: Research-fetch tools — `/research:arxiv <query|id>` searches the official arXiv API or fetches by id (title · authors · abstract · pdf); `/research:yt <url-or-id>` extracts YouTube caption transcript via the InnerTube ANDROID client. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_arxiv.hexa` / `_yt.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`); HTTP via curl, no API key.

plugins.name: gh-stack
plugins.description: Stacked-PR workflow skill — proposes `gh stack` commands (enabled repos) or the manual `gh pr create --base previous-layer` fallback. Encodes the <200-lines-per-layer · 1-concern governance.

plugins.name: paper
plugins.description: /paper <args> — arxiv-style LaTeX paper scaffolder. Verbs: new <slug> · sample <slug> · fig <size> <prompt> <out.png> (delegates to sister `imagine` → fal.ai `openai/gpt-image-2`) · compile [dir] (pdflatex × 3 + bibtex) · lint [dir] (enforces commons @D g51 — main.pdf ≥10 pages AND ≥1 fal.ai figure detected via figures/_prompts/*.{txt,md} or figures/_scripts/*.py grep for fal/imagine; exit 0 = pass) · list · help. Template is a minimal arxiv-style single-column 11pt skeleton — compiles end-to-end out of the box, one figure inlined. `fig` requires the sister `imagine` plugin.

plugins.name: imagine
plugins.description: /imagine <prompt-file> <out.png> [-s size] [-b backend] [-m model] — generic AI image generator. Two backends out of the box: `fal` (queue+poll fal.ai, default — `secret get fal.api_key`, model = openai/gpt-image-2 firm-pinned default) and `openai` (sync /v1/images/generations — `secret get openai.api_key`, model = gpt-image-1). Canonical fal-style size tokens (`square_hd` / `landscape_16_9` / `portrait_16_9` / `square`) translate per-backend. Prompt always read from a file (provenance), payload JSON via mktemp (prompt never on argv). Backend pluggable — drop a new `_backends/<name>.hexa` to add one.

plugins.name: output-trim
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) stdout trimmer — when a simple single Bash command would emit >8000 chars, transparently rewrite via `updatedInput` to pipe stdout through a minimal hexa-native kernel: consecutive-line dedup ([×N] counter) + per-line truncation to 200 chars. Small outputs <8000 chars pass verbatim. Skipped on any shell metacharacter so compound pipelines / redirects / heredocs / background jobs / command substitutions and other PreToolUse(Bash) rewrite hooks compose without conflict. Exit code preserved via `set -o pipefail`. Kernel chosen by benchmark on 120 real Bash-stdout samples ≥8000 chars — Pareto-dominates the TF-IDF salience + MinHash near-dup paragraph dedup approach of the original `wilson-output-trim`. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: subagent-route
plugins.description: [POC] PreToolUse(Task|Agent) observation hook + SessionStart observation-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_subagent_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). v0.1.0 is observation-only — it does NOT deny, NOT rewrite. On every Task/Agent fire it dumps `tool_input` (key list + agent_type + prompt preview + isolation) to `~/.sidecar/subagent-route.log.jsonl` (capped 100 lines, jsonl format mirroring pool-route's route-log) and emits a non-blocking `additionalContext` advisory suggesting the equivalent `ssh <host> -- claude --print --no-tty -p '<prompt>'` rewrite. SessionStart event reads the last 5 observations back as additionalContext so the model sees recent subagent fires without recon. PoC purpose — probe the actual tool_input payload shape at runtime (Claude Code docs leave Agent/Task tool_input fields under-specified vs Bash), and probe the `additionalContext` UX before a real-rewrite v0.2.0. Always exits 0; advisory only.

plugins.name: worktree-gc
plugins.description: SessionStart hook that prunes merged-but-undeleted LINKED git worktrees in the cwd repo, implemented in hexa-lang (`_worktree_gc.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Threshold gate (linked-count >= 3 OR disk avail < 20 GiB) keeps the common case (1-2 worktrees, ample disk) silent. Above threshold, fetches `gh pr list --state merged --limit 200` and prunes any linked worktree whose branch is in that merged set OR whose `origin/<branch>` ref is gone (force-removes the worktree directory and deletes the local branch). Open-PR worktrees are NEVER touched. gh unavailable / no merged PRs → short note + no action. Always exits 0 (non-blocking). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: sidecar-auto-sync
plugins.description: SessionStart hook that runs `sidecar sync` once per Claude Code session, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_auto_sync.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Closes the gap between an upstream `git push` on origin/main and the local plugin cache — no more manual `sidecar sync` between sessions. Silent on success; brief stderr advisory on failure. Skipped silently only on a real precondition: the `sidecar` binary not on PATH (fresh machine without `hx install sidecar` yet). Adds ~1-2s to session startup (network pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch). NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11) — no env var / config disable switch; uninstall the plugin for manual control.

plugins.name: s9-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for load-assessment commands (project.tape @D s9). Fires when a Bash command looks like a Mac load check — `uptime` (word) · `loadavg` / `vm.loadavg` (substring) · `ps -A` adjacency or `ps -Ao pcpu` · `top -l ` (macOS) / `top -b ` (Linux) · `getloadavg`. Emits non-blocking additionalContext reminding the agent that any conclusion drawn from raw load must EXCLUDE `claude` PIDs (claude sessions are exempt from local load attribution) and suggesting the canonical claude-excluded sum (`ps -Ao pcpu,comm | awk '$2 !~ /claude/ {s+=$1} END {print int(s)}'`). NEVER blocks — pure advisory. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_s9_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Ships per @D s7 (governance + enforcement together) — the @D s9 companion guard. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: step-by-step
plugins.description: /step-by-step:step-by-step (alias /step-by-step:sbs) — plan-first sequential runbook. Decomposes the task into an ordered, numbered plan, then executes every step strictly in order WITHOUT pausing between steps, narrating each step's start + ✅/⚠/❌ result. Halts only on a step failure (reports the failing step + verbatim error + un-run tail) or before a genuinely irreversible / destructive / outward-facing step (confirm-then-resume, same bar as the bypass self-check). The deliberate single-threaded counterpart to /cycle's parallel fan-out — one thread, top to bottom. Bare = the task in context; /step-by-step:step-by-step <task> targets explicitly.
